purpose: Defines the `!guide` command — an interactive command decision tree that helps users pick the right Synapse command for their situation. Displays a flowchart organized by intent (project setup, parallel work, monitoring, actions, resume, history, server, analysis, PKI, instrumentation, TOC, profiles, housekeeping), then a complete command reference table grouped by category, then a 'Quick Tips' section. Read-only static documentation rendered to terminal.
gotchas: Some category boxes are duplicated in the flowchart (e.g., 'Monitor a running swarm' appears twice) — this is the file as-shipped
The Project Workflow categories (PKI, Instrumentation, TOC, Profiles & discovery) are listed but each command's actual definition lives in _commands/project/, not Synapse/
Quick tips emphasize: !p_track is the default, !cancel-safe over !cancel, each chat uses its assigned dashboard
This is the only command in this directory that has no Steps section — it is purely declarative output
patterns: Three-block output: Decision Tree (intent flowchart) → Command Reference (categorical table) → Quick Tips
Decision tree uses ASCII tree art with ├── and └── connectors
Command reference is grouped by domain: Project Management, Swarm Lifecycle, Resume & Recovery, Monitoring, Server Control, Housekeeping, Project Analysis, PKI, TOC, Profiles & Discovery
conventions: Three numbered Steps each containing one large markdown block to display
Category headers use ### inside the displayed flowchart
Each command shown with backticked syntax in the reference table
